roadHighlights: Blue Ridge Parkway winding through the Appalachians, plus Tail of the Dragon (318 curves in 11 miles) just 90 minutes away.
Trail Ridge Road through Rocky Mountain National Park, plus countless mountain passes like Guanella Pass and Loveland Pass.
Columbia River Gorge Historic Highway, Mt. Hood Loop with sweeping volcanic views, and the Oregon Coast scenic byway.
Oak Creek Canyon (AZ-89A) is one of the most dramatic drives in the Southwest, plus Verde Valley scenic roads.
Great Smoky Mountains US-441, Fighting Creek Gap, and easy access to Tail of the Dragon at Deals Gap.
17-Mile Drive, Pacific Coast Highway south toward Big Sur — arguably the most scenic coastal road in the US.
Beartooth Highway (called 'the most beautiful road in America'), Gallatin Canyon, and the approach to Yellowstone.
High Road to Taos through mountain villages, the Turquoise Trail (NM-14) — classic Southwest mountain driving.
Skyline Drive through Shenandoah National Park — 105 miles of ridgeline views with no traffic lights.
Highway 89 around Lake Tahoe, Emerald Bay loop, and the Monitor Pass — Nevada and California's best Sierra Nevada roads.
Vermont Route 100 down the spine of the Green Mountains — the quintessential fall foliage driving road.
Cascade Lakes Scenic Byway looping around volcanic peaks, McKenzie Pass through ancient lava fields.
M-22 coastal loop around Leelanau Peninsula — Michigan's most beloved scenic drive along Lake Michigan shoreline.
Needles Highway with its narrow rock tunnels and spires, Iron Mountain Road with Mt. Rushmore pigtail bridges.
Valley of Fire loop, Red Rock Canyon Scenic Drive, and the access road to Death Valley — diverse desert driving.
Sandia Crest Scenic Byway (NM-536) climbing 10,678 ft, Jemez Mountain Trail through red rock canyons.
Bitterroot Valley US-93, the approach to Glacier National Park, and the Lolo Pass — big sky country driving.
AZ-89A through Oak Creek Canyon to Sedona, San Francisco Peaks Scenic Road, and Oak Creek Canyon.
Georgia coastal scenic drives, the Lowcountry's mossy oak canopy roads, and South Carolina's ACE Basin byway.
Direct access to Tail of the Dragon (US-129) — the world's most famous motorcycle and sports car road with 318 curves.
Angeles Crest Highway (CA-2) through the San Gabriels, Mulholland Drive, and PCH north toward Malibu and Ventura.
Sunrise Highway up the Laguna Mountains, Palomar Mountain Road, and the back roads of Anza-Borrego desert.
Highway 1 south to Half Moon Bay and Big Sur, Marin Headlands twisties, and the Panoramic Highway over Mt. Tamalpais.
